title: Cutoff-independent continuum limit of the perturbative correlators (conditional)
statement: Under the SPECTRAL (Galerkin) regulator G_a(q)=1_{|q|<=pi/a}/K(q) and gamma>0 (measure well-defined), every connected perturbative amplitude of the scalar Brazovskii theory (d=3, mu^2>0) converges lim_{a->0} A_{G,a}(p)=A_G(p) for each FIXED external momentum p (dominated convergence: the spectral bound G_a(q)<=C(1+|q|)^-4 holds for ALL unwrapped q_i + Weinberg integrability), with Lambda=pi/a tying a->0==Lambda->inf; hence each order of the perturbative correlators has a cutoff-independent continuum limit. The genuine finite-difference lattice (folded momenta / Umklapp) is the separate Route-B refinement.
scope: scalar, d=3, mu^2>0, gamma>0, PERTURBATIVE order-by-order, SPECTRAL/Galerkin regulator (Route A). The finite-difference lattice qhat=(2/a)sin(aq/2) of v1.1/v1.2 was REFUTED for the domination (qhat is periodic => internal-line momenta fold => the (1+|q|)^-4 bound fails on Umklapp/aliasing); the spectral regulator carries the continuum kernel + a sharp per-line cutoff and closes it. Route B (genuine lattice, Reisz power counting) is a separate harder refinement. gamma>0 measure hypothesis (A2-H2-SEXTIC-COERCIVITY). NOT resummation; NOT constructive (A3_constructive); NOT full Class-II; NOT mu^2<0. Convergence is for each FIXED external momentum p (uniform-on-compact-p would need a separate equicontinuity argument; not claimed). Kernel hypothesis Y>0 (4th-order ellipticity / q^-4 decay) is made explicit in the consolidation v1.1.
falsifier: A connected graph G with A_{G,a} not-> A_G under the spectral regulator; or sup_q (1+|q|)^4/K(q) infinite; or gamma<=0 (measure undefined).
no_overclaim: A ratified T6 (operator verdict is T3 PROOF SKETCH pending ratification of the lemma + the gamma>0 measure hypothesis); order-by-order only (NOT resummation); NOT constructive/non-perturbative; NOT full Class-II; NOT mu^2<0.
